{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resource Officer",
  "description": "Nobel-Eagle Industries Limited is a dynamic manufacturing powerhouse with a diverse portfolio spanning edible oils, bottled water, animal feeds, breakfast cereals, palm kernel oil, and seasoning cubes. Noble-Eagle is a formidable industrial entity that seamlessly integrates various production lines and delivers exceptional products that cater to the evolving needs of local and global markets.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Owo, Ondo
Employment Type: Full-time
Reporting Line:&nbsp;Reports to the HR Business Partner

Description


	The HR Officer will support the effective delivery of human resource operations across the organization.
	The role involves recruitment, employee relations, performance management, and HR administration, ensuring alignment with company policies and regulatory requirements.


Key Responsibilities


	Support end-to-end recruitment processes including job postings, screening, interviews, and onboarding
	Maintain and update employee records, HR databases, and documentation
	Assist in the implementation of HR policies and procedures
	Handle employee relations matters, including grievances, disciplinary processes, and conflict resolution
	Monitor employee attendance, leave records, and ensure proper documentation
	Support performance management processes, including appraisals and feedback tracking
	Coordinate staff training and development initiatives
	Ensure compliance with labor laws and company policies
	Support payroll processes by ensuring accurate and timely employee data
	Prepare HR reports and provide insights to management.


Requirements:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field
	Minimum of 3 years&rsquo; relevant HR experience
	Good knowledge of HR practices and Nigerian labor laws
	Strong interpersonal and communication skills
	Good organizational and administrative abilities
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and HRIS tools
	High level of integrity and confidentiality.


Added Advantage:


	Experience working in a manufacturing or factory environment.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;o.ovakporaye@capitalsage.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Human Resource Officer Job at Nobel-Eagle Industries Limited




        
    

        
        Nobel-Eagle Industries Limited is a dynamic manufacturing powerhouse with a diverse portfolio spanning edible oils, bottled water, animal feeds, breakfast cereals, palm kernel oil, and seasoning cubes. Noble-Eagle is a formidable industrial entity that seamlessly integrates various production lines and delivers exceptional products that cater to the evolving needs of local and global markets.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Officer

Job Location: Owo, Ondo
Employment Type: Full-time
Reporting Line: Reports to the HR Business Partner

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The HR Officer will support the effective delivery of human resource operations across the organization.
	The role involves recruitment, employee relations, performance management, and HR administration, ensuring alignment with company policies and regulatory requirements.


Key Responsibilities


	Support end-to-end recruitment processes including job postings, screening, interviews, and onboarding
	Maintain and update employee records, HR databases, and documentation
	Assist in the implementation of HR policies and procedures
	Handle employee relations matters, including grievances, disciplinary processes, and conflict resolution
	Monitor employee attendance, leave records, and ensure proper documentation
	Support performance management processes, including appraisals and feedback tracking
	Coordinate staff training and development initiatives
	Ensure compliance with labor laws and company policies
	Support payroll processes by ensuring accurate and timely employee data
	Prepare HR reports and provide insights to management.


Requirements:


	Bachelor’s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field
	Minimum of 3 years’ relevant HR experience
	Good knowledge of HR practices and Nigerian labor laws
	Strong interpersonal and communication skills
	Good organizational and administrative abilities
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and HRIS tools
	High level of integrity and confidentiality.


Added Advantage:


	Experience working in a manufacturing or factory environment.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Rig Supervisor III - Senior",
  "description": "Scruples Resources Limited is a Premier Oil and Gas Support Services Provider. Established in Lagos, Nigeria, Scruples Resources Limited stands as a distinguished leader in the oil and gas industry. We specialize in delivering comprehensive support services, encompassing consultancy, logistics, procurement, engineering, manpower provisioning, and personnel training.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Rig Supervisor III - Senior Position

Job Location:&nbsp;Oso, Akwa Ibom
Employment Type: Contract

Job Description


	Represents the company on a rig site to support the safe and efficient execution of well plans.
	Is the primary contact for rig and third party personnel to ensure logistics for personnel, equipment and services are appropriately organized and managed.


Tasks &amp;&nbsp;Responsibilities


	Responsible for communicating expectations regarding safety, security, health, and environment; provides guidance and supports managing Safety, Security, Health, and Environment (SSHE) events.
	Responsible for communicating oi Systems (oi System (oi) ) expectations on the rig site.
	Monitors rig operations to provide feedback and input to the rig contractor to ensure well control procedures are properly followed and emergency situations are appropriately managed.
	Responsible for ensuring that safety, operational, environmental, security and other drills are conducted on site at specified frequencies.
	Responsible for ensuring drilling and completion procedures are followed as approved and utilizes management of change processes if changes are required.
	Orders and organizes logistics for services and equipment necessary to conduct drilling and completion operations efficiently &bull; Supports onsite surveillance requirements for the operation in areas such as kick detection, abnormal pressure detection, torque &amp; drag, mud properties, and solids control efficiency.
	Responsible for generation of accurate and adequate operations reports.
	Interfaces with engineering and geology to ensure understanding of well objectives and procedural requirements.


Skills and Qualifications


	Experience in rig supervisory or rig leadership roles for drilling and completion operations.
	Experience working for a major Operator or Drilling Contractor.
	Hold current certification for Well Control.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and and qualified candidates should send their updated CV to:&nbsp;recruitmentscruplesresourceltd@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note


	Do not send your certificate, training certification, NYSC, SSCE, B.Sc certificate; all these are not needed, only your CV.
	Please carefully review the job requirements, skills, and job location before applying. Candidates whose CVs do not align with the job description will be disqualified from the recruitment process.
	It is essential to pay attention to the job description!
	Any candidate who submits their CV without following this instruction will be disqualified and not shortlisted.
	Important:Candidates must have at least 3 years of experience in the oil and gas industry. Any candidates who apply without this experience will have their CVs rejected immediately.
	Only qualified candidates who meet the requirements for this position will be contacted.
	Therefore, please ensure your CV aligns with the role before applying. Do not apply just for the sake of applying.
	Take your time, align your CV with the job requirements and specifications before submitting it.This is very important.

Rig Supervisor III - Senior Job Position at Scruples Resources Limited




        
    

        
        Scruples Resources Limited is a Premier Oil and Gas Support Services Provider. Established in Lagos, Nigeria, Scruples Resources Limited stands as a distinguished leader in the oil and gas industry. We specialize in delivering comprehensive support services, encompassing consultancy, logistics, procurement, engineering, manpower provisioning, and personnel training.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Rig Supervisor III - Senior Position

Job Location: Oso, Akwa Ibom
Employment Type: Contract

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Represents the company on a rig site to support the safe and efficient execution of well plans.
	Is the primary contact for rig and third party personnel to ensure logistics for personnel, equipment and services are appropriately organized and managed.


Tasks & Responsibilities


	Responsible for communicating expectations regarding safety, security, health, and environment; provides guidance and supports managing Safety, Security, Health, and Environment (SSHE) events.
	Responsible for communicating oi Systems (oi System (oi) ) expectations on the rig site.
	Monitors rig operations to provide feedback and input to the rig contractor to ensure well control procedures are properly followed and emergency situations are appropriately managed.
	Responsible for ensuring that safety, operational, environmental, security and other drills are conducted on site at specified frequencies.
	Responsible for ensuring drilling and completion procedures are followed as approved and utilizes management of change processes if changes are required.
	Orders and organizes logistics for services and equipment necessary to conduct drilling and completion operations efficiently • Supports onsite surveillance requirements for the operation in areas such as kick detection, abnormal pressure detection, torque & drag, mud properties, and solids control efficiency.
	Responsible for generation of accurate and adequate operations reports.
	Interfaces with engineering and geology to ensure understanding of well objectives and procedural requirements.


Skills and Qualifications


	Experience in rig supervisory or rig leadership roles for drilling and completion operations.
	Experience working for a major Operator or Drilling Contractor.
	Hold current certification for Well Control.


Method of Application 
Interested and and qualified candidates should send their updated CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note


	Do not send your certificate, training certification, NYSC, SSCE, B.Sc certificate; all these are not needed, only your CV.
	Please carefully review the job requirements, skills, and job location before applying. Candidates whose CVs do not align with the job description will be disqualified from the recruitment process.
	It is essential to pay attention to the job description!
	Any candidate who submits their CV without following this instruction will be disqualified and not shortlisted.
	Important:Candidates must have at least 3 years of experience in the oil and gas industry. Any candidates who apply without this experience will have their CVs rejected immediately.
	Only qualified candidates who meet the requirements for this position will be contacted.
	Therefore, please ensure your CV aligns with the role before applying. Do not apply just for the sake of applying.
	Take your time, align your CV with the job requirements and specifications before submitting it.This is very important.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

	2&ndash;4 years of experience in energy sales
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills

Sales Specialist Job at Itel Energy




        
    

        
        Itel Energy is a renewable energy solutions company focused on providing reliable, affordable, and sustainable solar power solutions across Nigeria. As part of the itel ecosystem, Itel Energy leverages strong distribution networks and deep market reach to deliver innovative off-grid and hybrid solar solutions for homes, SMEs, and commercial customers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Specialist

Job Locations: Kwara & Ekiti
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Sales Specialist will be responsible for driving sales and revenue growth by promoting the company’s energy solutions to customers.
	The role involves identifying new sales opportunities, engaging customers, and providing tailored energy solutions such as solar systems, batteries, and power backup products.
	The ideal candidate is customer-focused, target-driven, and has experience in sales, preferably within the energy or technology sector.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and pursue new sales opportunities within assigned territories
	Promote and sell Itel Energy’s products, including solar solutions, batteries, and power systems
	Build and maintain strong relationships with new and existing customers
	Conduct product presentations and demonstrations to prospective clients
	Understand customer needs and recommend suitable energy solutions
	Achieve and exceed assigned sales targets and KPIs
	Maintain accurate records of sales activities and customer interactions
	Provide timely reports on sales performance and market feedback
	Collaborate with internal teams to ensure customer satisfaction and timely delivery


Requirements & Qualifications


	Bachelor’s degree or HND in Business, Marketing, or related field
	2–4 years of experience in energy sales
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Proven ability to meet or exceed sales targets
	Ability to work independently and manage time effectively
	Basic knowledge of solar or energy products is an added advantage
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Operations Manager (Construction)",
  "description": "The Paradigm Group is a dynamic, multidisciplinary organization headquartered in Abuja, Nigeria, with a strong focus on delivering innovative and high-quality solutions across architecture, construction, and real estate development.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Operations Manager (Construction)

Job Location: Kano

Job Summary


	The Operations Manager (Construction) is responsible for overseeing and optimizing all operational activities related to project execution, site management, procurement, and resource coordination.
	This role ensures that all construction projects are delivered on time, within budget, and to the highest quality standards.
	The ideal candidate must have strong knowledge of construction processes, exceptional attention to detail, and the ability to build and enforce structured systems that drive efficiency, accountability, and performance across all operational units.


Key Responsibilities
Project &amp; Site Operations Oversight:


	Oversee end-to-end construction operations across all active project sites
	Ensure alignment between design intent and site execution
	Monitor project timelines, milestones, and deliverables
	Conduct regular site visits and performance reviews
	Identify and resolve operational bottlenecks proactively.


Planning, Scheduling &amp; Execution Control:


	Develop and monitor project execution plans and schedules
	Ensure proper sequencing of construction activities
	Track progress against approved timelines and escalate delays
	Coordinate with Project Managers, Architects, and Site Supervisors.


Budgeting, Cost Control &amp; Financial Oversight:


	Work closely with Quantity Surveyors to review BOQs and cost estimates
	Monitor project budgets and ensure strict cost control
	Prevent cost overruns through proactive planning and tracking
	Approve operational expenditures in line with company policies.


Procurement &amp; Supply Chain Management:


	Oversee procurement processes for materials and services
	Ensure timely sourcing and delivery of construction materials
	Evaluate and manage vendors, suppliers, and contractors
	Implement structured procurement and inventory systems.


Quality Assurance &amp; Compliance:


	Ensure all construction activities meet quality standards and specifications
	Enforce compliance with building codes, safety regulations, and company policies
	Implement quality control procedures across all projects
	Conduct periodic audits and inspections.


Process Development &amp; Operational Efficiency:


	Design and implement Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for operations
	Drive process standardization across sites and departments
	Identify inefficiencies and implement improvement strategies
	Introduce systems for tracking performance and reporting.


Team Leadership &amp; Coordination:


	Supervise and coordinate operational teams across multiple sites
	Ensure clear role definition and accountability for all team members
	Provide leadership, direction, and performance management.
	Foster a culture of discipline, structure, and high performance.


Reporting &amp; Stakeholder Management:


	Prepare and present operational reports to management
	Provide updates on project progress, risks, and financial performance
	Collaborate with senior leadership on strategic decisions
	Maintain strong communication across all departments.


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Architecture, Civil Engineering, Building Technology, or related fields
	5 - 8 years of experience in construction operations or project management
	Strong knowledge of construction processes, site management, and project execution
	Proven experience managing multiple construction projects simultaneously
	Strong understanding of budgeting, procurement, and cost control
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and project management tools
	Knowledge of Nigerian construction regulations and standards


Key Competencies:


	Strong attention to detail
	Strategic thinking with execution focus
	Leadership and team management
	High level of accountability
	Problem-solving and decision-making ability
	Strong organizational and coordination skills
	Financial and operational discipline


What We Are Looking For (Very Important):


	A structured thinker, not just a supervisor
	Someone who can build systems, not just manage people
	A professional who understands both site realities and operational strategy
	A leader who drives discipline, accountability, and efficiency.


What We Offer


	Salary: N200,000 - N300,000 monthly.
	Opportunity to lead operations in a growing, structured firm
	Exposure to high-impact construction projects
	Career growth into senior leadership roles
	Professional and performance-driven work environment


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@theparadigmgroup.com.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as subject of email.

Note


	At The Paradigm Architects, we are building a system-driven organization where operations are not left to chance.
	The Operations Manager plays a critical role in ensuring that our projects are executed with precision, discipline, and excellence.
",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-26",

Operations Manager (Construction) Job at the Paradigm Group




        
    

        
        The Paradigm Group is a dynamic, multidisciplinary organization headquartered in Abuja, Nigeria, with a strong focus on delivering innovative and high-quality solutions across architecture, construction, and real estate development.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Manager (Construction)

Job Location: Kano

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Operations Manager (Construction) is responsible for overseeing and optimizing all operational activities related to project execution, site management, procurement, and resource coordination.
	This role ensures that all construction projects are delivered on time, within budget, and to the highest quality standards.
	The ideal candidate must have strong knowledge of construction processes, exceptional attention to detail, and the ability to build and enforce structured systems that drive efficiency, accountability, and performance across all operational units.


Key Responsibilities
Project & Site Operations Oversight:


	Oversee end-to-end construction operations across all active project sites
	Ensure alignment between design intent and site execution
	Monitor project timelines, milestones, and deliverables
	Conduct regular site visits and performance reviews
	Identify and resolve operational bottlenecks proactively.


Planning, Scheduling & Execution Control:


	Develop and monitor project execution plans and schedules
	Ensure proper sequencing of construction activities
	Track progress against approved timelines and escalate delays
	Coordinate with Project Managers, Architects, and Site Supervisors.


Budgeting, Cost Control & Financial Oversight:


	Work closely with Quantity Surveyors to review BOQs and cost estimates
	Monitor project budgets and ensure strict cost control
	Prevent cost overruns through proactive planning and tracking
	Approve operational expenditures in line with company policies.


Procurement & Supply Chain Management:


	Oversee procurement processes for materials and services
	Ensure timely sourcing and delivery of construction materials
	Evaluate and manage vendors, suppliers, and contractors
	Implement structured procurement and inventory systems.


Quality Assurance & Compliance:


	Ensure all construction activities meet quality standards and specifications
	Enforce compliance with building codes, safety regulations, and company policies
	Implement quality control procedures across all projects
	Conduct periodic audits and inspections.


Process Development & Operational Efficiency:


	Design and implement Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for operations
	Drive process standardization across sites and departments
	Identify inefficiencies and implement improvement strategies
	Introduce systems for tracking performance and reporting.


Team Leadership & Coordination:


	Supervise and coordinate operational teams across multiple sites
	Ensure clear role definition and accountability for all team members
	Provide leadership, direction, and performance management.
	Foster a culture of discipline, structure, and high performance.


Reporting & Stakeholder Management:


	Prepare and present operational reports to management
	Provide updates on project progress, risks, and financial performance
	Collaborate with senior leadership on strategic decisions
	Maintain strong communication across all departments.


Requirements & Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Architecture, Civil Engineering, Building Technology, or related fields
	5 - 8 years of experience in construction operations or project management
	Strong knowledge of construction processes, site management, and project execution
	Proven experience managing multiple construction projects simultaneously
	Strong understanding of budgeting, procurement, and cost control
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and project management tools
	Knowledge of Nigerian construction regulations and standards


Key Competencies:


	Strong attention to detail
	Strategic thinking with execution focus
	Leadership and team management
	High level of accountability
	Problem-solving and decision-making ability
	Strong organizational and coordination skills
	Financial and operational discipline


What We Are Looking For (Very Important):


	A structured thinker, not just a supervisor
	Someone who can build systems, not just manage people
	A professional who understands both site realities and operational strategy
	A leader who drives discipline, accountability, and efficiency.


What We Offer


	Salary: N200,000 - N300,000 monthly.
	Opportunity to lead operations in a growing, structured firm
	Exposure to high-impact construction projects
	Career growth into senior leadership roles
	Professional and performance-driven work environment


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as subject of email.

Note


	At The Paradigm Architects, we are building a system-driven organization where operations are not left to chance.
	The Operations Manager plays a critical role in ensuring that our projects are executed with precision, discipline, and excellence.

Employment Type: Full-time

	Coordinate general repairs and service of office equipment and appliances
	Monitor the use of company vehicles.
	Supervise repairs of all company vehicles
	Ensure renewal of maintenance service level agreement for copier and generator.
	Ensure payment of rent from tenant and payment of rent of office spaces.

	Proven experience in administrative management or a similar role.
	Strong leadership and organizational skills.
	Good communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to multitask and manage office operations effective.

Admin Officer Job at Lington & Bernie Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        Lington & Bernie Consulting Limited was established on October 8, 1999 to provide efficient and qualitative consulting services to the oil and gas industry as well as banks and financial institutions in the areas of human resources placement and management, human resources consulting services, tax administration and payroll services as well as oil and gas fiscal and contractual terms advisory services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Admin Officer

Job Location: CMS, Lagos Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Coordinate general repairs and service of office equipment and appliances
	Monitor the use of company vehicles.
	Supervise repairs of all company vehicles
	Ensure renewal of maintenance service level agreement for copier and generator.
	Ensure payment of rent from tenant and payment of rent of office spaces.


Requirement


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Management, or related fields
	Proven experience in administrative management or a similar role.
	Strong leadership and organizational skills.
	Good communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to multitask and manage office operations effective.


Salary
N150,000 - N200,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
